name: Fennel Gratin with Robiola
recipeIngredient: 2 oz fresh bread crumbs, lightly toasted under broiler
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
2. Bring 4 quarts water to boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
3. Butter 4 small round (4-inch diameter) earthenware gratin or shallow sauce dishes.
4. Halve fennel bulbs and cut into 1/4-inch thick slices.
5. Place in boiling water and blanche until very tender, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain in a colander over sink and set aside until cool enough to handle.
6. Mix tepid fennel with bechamel sauce and fontina cheese and mix well.
7. Divide evenly among 4 gratin dishes and pat down with back of spoon.
8. Bake in top half of oven for 25 minutes until bubbling and hot.
9. Remove from oven. Place 2 ounces (dollop or square) of robiola in center of each dish, sprinkle bread crumbs over robiola and place in oven 5 to 6 more minutes, until robiola is hot and soft and crumbs have melted.
10. Remove. Allow to stand 3 minutes before serving.
Yield: 4 servings
